passages.excerpt:
Then Judaism is even more occluded than Christianity, because by denying or ignoring the Fall Judaism has not realization that man has lost some of his faculties entirely and has had others occluded (impaired). The punishment by YHWH is totally successful, and part of the saving gnosis is contained in the Christian doctrine of the Fall because it permits us to comprehend that we are impaired -- deprived of some of our original faculties. So already, by believing in the Fall, Christianity has rolled back the cloud of enforced ignorance which the demiurge covered us with.

We fell, and the idea is to regain what we lost (1) in the way of faculties; and (2) return to Eden. "The Golden Age of humanity is not in the past but in the future," Rabbi Herz writes in his notes on Genesis. Well, and (3): the fruit of the Tree of (eternal) Life, which Christ specifically offers us.

Then I can see the conventional or orthodox Christianity in itself contains much of the saving gnosis, irrespective of Gnosticism. We fell; we lost Eden; we lost our faculties; Christ restores us (as Milton says; the doctrine of the Second Adam who undoes the work of the first). Judaism knows absolutely nothing of this, of the events (loss of faculties) and aim (restoration). So of course, the revelation at Mt. Sinai is considered adequate. And of course in Christianity it is not, and Christ must tell us, "But I say unto you --" etc, and then so does. If you study Judaism and contrast it to Christianity you can see, for the first time, that Christianity is Faustian, that its real but covert message is, "We were once as gods or as God, and Christ shall restore us," ideas totally absent in Judaism, as is also absent the concept that the

folder 82 - 024

So the doctrine of original sin is a method that permits Christians to believe all this without assigning any blame to God for man's Fall. In no sense did God do wrong in blighting, expelling and impairing and punishing man. Gnosticism sees this as a tactic and rejects it. The doctrine of original sin is only necessary if man alone is to hold the place of the guilty party, but the Gnostics are willing to blame God.

What is my opinion? Well, I have the statement of the AI voice. "Stolen Secret." This is absolutely Gnostic. Also, my "messenger" dream is Gnostic (as well as other elements; many, in fact). However, you have to become a dualist to be a Gnostic; you have to believe in two gods, not one. That's the price you pay for abandoning the doctrine of original sin. Paul could retain his monotheism. The Gnostics can't and don't want to.

Christianity is very wise; it stresses the remedy above the malady in its "felix culpa" doctrine -- which insures the positive or Faustian aspect of the belief-system, rather than the negative aspect (fallen, debased, sinful man... although many Protestants stressed the latter -- but it is said of them that they went back to the O.T.; after all, kerygma or gospel means "good news" not "bad news"). The concept of "felix culpa" is not tangential to Christianity but cardinal. In a sense everything is expressed here;

he "I" is precisely what it is willing to sacrifice and does sacrifice. 'veil, then, has the "I" revolted in a Faustian manner, against this impersonal will? Is this what is meant by, ''The secret stolen through the angels in one’s hands"'.' The revolt of the ontogenic self equals "Secret stolen," which may also refer to the primordial act of rebellion which brought on the occlusion and enslavement and death: the eating of the fruit of the tree of knowledge (of good and evil, which obviously represents free will and individual decision-making; which is to say individuality, ontology itself). Then is the removal of the occlusion, the abo¬ lition of the death strip, the fighting off DNA enslavement, which is to say the Umwelt — is this a rebellion by the canny individual "I" who grasps the fact that to the DNA will, it —-the "I"— is expendable? And it rebels against this expandability? a further act of disobedience — hence trie expression "secret stolen"; a double reference to the forbidden: (1) It is secret presumably from us in the first place; and (2) someone steals it and puts it in our hands, having smuggled it "through the angels," which quite obviously are Paul’s thrones, powers, dominions, etc.; the ancient ruling forces whom God appointed at the beginning.

"Secret solen through (i.e. past) the angels." Not "stolen from God." Could the angels have become an entrenched tyranny whom God now dethrones through the activity of Christ? This precisely was Paul’s view; he saw the ancient angelic powers as having become tyrannical; he equated them with astral determinism and viewed Christ as pitted against their rule — successfully pitted; that salvation had to do with an abolition of their rule

II

630

He has a sudden, unexpected advantage over Fate: he has recognized it for what it really is + because of this recognition + because of what it really is, he has decisive power over it. Their relative positions of power are now reversed, if he will only act. This is a Faustian conception of man, + a reinterpretation of the true purpose of religion: to provide one with this decisive power (i.e. to save or extricate or free or enlighten or awaken or cause a rebirth, etc.; the phrasing varies) over Fate or the world or death. (Or sin etc.) (Or Satan.) In my formulation Xtian salvation, Sankara's "Tat tvam asi" + Buddhist Moksa + release from the wheel, + Gnostic triumph over heimarmene, the same with the mystery religions - all are implied - it underlies them all. Since each person has only one Fate, this moment comes once + once only in a lifetime. He must act or die. + if he listens in the silence of the night he will hear the voice of his own heroic self, his own pure intelligence, speaking to him, telling him what is the true state of things, since his heroic self pitilessly sees things as they

It will be as if every combination of message units has been tried out (every possible meta-morpheme formed + then uncoupled in favor of another; a point comes where there is no other. At this point the mind of the system has complete, perfect self-knowledge, + since nothing exists outside it, everything that can be an object of knowledge has in fact become an object of knowledge. Being + being known, then, are regarded as one + the same. Viz: "God does not know us because we exist; we exist because he knows us.") The BIP, then, is a timeless perennial specter always intruding on process creation; it always is + it never changes, + unless actively combatted it automatically wins (since, as I say, in a certain real sense it is the natural state or outcome of things in motion). At every forking junction it remanifests itself by default. Thus to perceive it -as I did- is to see into the timeless, + to see one's absolute foe, the foe of creation (viewed as growth-process, as something evolving toward greater integration + complexity). So two supratemporal constants emerge: the BIP + those combatting it, eternal revolutionaries under a variety of names. This, perceived, is the dialectic itself perceived, in terms of history (which is the arena of process). (The BIP is non-process: it is the death of process. + since God + process are essentially related -process is God completing himself- the BIP is the totality of the no or sameness stages added up + the aggregate is antithetical to God's nature - this is what all the "nos" add up to: the potential death of God. One is tempted to say, then, that God is Faustian.

on the totality of the previous stages (the MMSK); hence this is a truly Faustian mental entity. Contrast this with the utter rigidity of psyche A in which time is non-cumulative (cyclic) & even with psyche B (where time is linear - i.e. cumulative - but lacks the dialectic to drive it on in the manner expemplified in Beethoven's music in which each new composition suprasses & subsumes all - the totality of - the previous compositions; this is internal evolution. & its time is a new kind of time, to linear time as linear time is to cyclic time. I call it "synchronous" time since it is acausal.) (Viz: the future stage determines the past, not vice versa; it may even be a sort of retrograde or teleological time.) Thus Ditheon itself imposes itself onto the B psyche rather than evolving out of it, from it, as if flowing into it to use it for its own purposes. For the type A psyche the past rules. For type B the present. For type C, Ditheon, the future rules & tugs the present as a perturbation of the reality field. So rather than evolution there is an igniting or catalytic effect, as if it is triggered off from latency to active mode.

Though there is bilateral hemispheric parity, psyche B (the wise horn) is smarter than psych A (the stupider horn). Nonetheless both are equally essential to the total unity. Type A is the Father, hence power. Type B the Son: wisdom. Type C: Love.
